1.description: Returns of UC and euro funds. This figure plots the average monthly returns of euro funds and UC funds in our sample period, from September 2002 to April 2011. [Color figure can be viewed atwileyonlinelibrary.com]

2.description: Financial literacy and portfolio returns. This figure plots annual returns (in %) over our seven-point financial literacy index. The middle solid line corresponds to linear estimates; the upper and lower solid lines show the 95% confidence interval. The dotted line corresponds to nonparametric estimates through local polynomial regressions (local-mean smoothing estimated with the Epanechnikov kernel and the rule-of-thumb bandwidth). [Color figure can be viewed atwileyonlinelibrary.com]

3.description: Risk taking and market returns over time. This figure plotsDifference in Risky ShareandMarket Returnsin our sample period, from September 2002 to April 2011.Difference in Risky Shareis the difference between the average risky share at the end of montht−1for households with financial literacy above the median in our sample (equal to 4) and the average risky share for those with below-median financial literacy.Market Returnsis the difference between the average return of risky assets and that of riskless assets in montht. [Color figure can be viewed atwileyonlinelibrary.com]

4.description: Risk taking and market returns. On the vertical axis,Difference in Risky Shareis the difference between the average risky share at the end of montht−1 for households with financial literacy above the median in our sample (equal to 4) and the average risky share for those with below-median financial literacy. On the horizontal axis,Market Returnsis the difference between the average return of risky assets and that of riskless assets in montht. The dots correspond to the observed relation in our sample period, the middle solid line corresponds to the linear fit, and the upper and lower solid lines plot the 95% confidence interval. [Color figure can be viewed atwileyonlinelibrary.com]

5.description: Change in risk exposure over time. This figure plots the change in the risky shareΔXi,tover time through local polynomial regressions (local-mean smoothing estimated with the Epanechnikov kernel and the rule-of-thumb bandwidth). The sample is split into two groups.High Literacyrefers to households with financial literacy above the median in our sample (equal to 4), whereasLow Literacyrefers to households with financial literacy below the median. [Color figure can be viewed atwileyonlinelibrary.com]
